name: CEO / Managing Director
description: CEOs and managing directors of Dutch mid-market companies (€10M-€500M) who sense their company has unrealized potential but lack the time or framework to step back and recalibrate strategy. Often pulled into daily operations.
painPoints: Strategy exists in fragments across the organization — no unified direction
Pulled into operations, unable to step back for strategic thinking
Multiple business units or initiatives without clear structural alignment
Uncertainty about competitive positioning (generalist vs specialist)
motivations: Want clarity on where the company stands and where it should go
Need a framework their leadership team can rally around
Value directness and pragmatism over consultant jargon
Seek quick, tangible results — not multi-month engagements
preferredTone: Direct, confident, data-backed. No fluff. Show the framework, show the evidence.

name: Board Member / Supervisory Board
description: Board members and supervisory board directors who need confidence that the company's strategic direction is coherent and well-documented. Often concerned about governance, oversight, and strategic alignment.
painPoints: Cannot easily assess whether strategy is coherent across business units
Strategy documentation is inconsistent or incomplete
Lack of measurable strategic objectives and monitoring frameworks
Concerned about execution gap between strategy and operations
motivations: Want board-ready assessments with clear scoring and benchmarks
Need confidence that strategic direction is evidence-based
Value structured frameworks over intuition-based strategy
preferredTone: Analytical, structured, evidence-heavy. Reference benchmarks and research.

name: Business Unit Leader
description: Leaders of individual business units or divisions within mid-market companies who need to understand how their unit's strategy connects to the overall direction. Often running their own initiatives without clear alignment to group strategy.
painPoints: Unclear how their unit's strategy relates to the overall company strategy
Different units using different frameworks and documentation styles
Need to justify resource allocation for their unit's initiatives
motivations: Want clear alignment between unit strategy and group direction
Need a shared language and framework for strategic discussions
Value practical tools (strategy house, must-win battles) over abstract theory
Seek clarity on prioritization — what are the must-win battles
preferredTone: Practical, visual, action-oriented. Show how pieces fit together.

name: Founder / Owner-Operator
description: Founders and owner-operators of growing Dutch companies who built the business on entrepreneurial instinct and now need to formalize and scale their strategic direction. Typically 5-15 years in, facing scaling challenges.
painPoints: Strategy lives in the founder's head, not in a transferable document
Growing beyond the point where personal oversight covers everything
Need to professionalize without losing entrepreneurial culture
Hiring leaders who need clear strategic direction to execute independently
motivations: Want to preserve what makes the company special while scaling
Need a strategic framework that empowers team autonomy
Value the co-creative partnership model — not being told what to do
Seek a consultant who respects the entrepreneurial culture
preferredTone: Conversational, respectful of what they've built, forward-looking. Emphasize co-creation.
